text: Risk is one of the basic and inalienable attributes of any human activity. In business, it can not be completely eliminated. Therefore, it can only be reduced to a minimum, while its harmful effects are minimized under certain conditions. The risk classification is a very wide field. In scientific literature, a number of risk classifications are presented and discussed and the purpose of the classification is always reflected in the method of risk classification. Risk categorisation means to classify the file of a risk into one of the pre-defined categories based on the parameters of the respective risk. In the article, the authors present a summarised review of the theoretical aspects of risk categorisation and especially the consequences of risks connected with business. The aim of the article is to give an overview of the theoretical risk classifications that contribute to the Risk Management discipline as a scientific discipline.
